title: skopeo inspect is failing.  No signature storage configuration found for myregistry.com:5000/ubuntu:18.04
body:
[anchore@ anchore-engine]$ skopeo --debug inspect --creds anchore:anchore docker://myregistry.com:5000/ubuntu:18.04
DEBU[0000] reference rewritten from 'myregistry.com:5000/ubuntu:18.04' to 'myregistry.com:5000/ubuntu:18.04' 
DEBU[0000] Trying to access "myregistry.com:5000/ubuntu:18.04" 
DEBU[0000] Returning credentials from DockerAuthConfig  
DEBU[0000] Using registries.d directory /etc/containers/registries.d for sigstore configuration 
DEBU[0000]  No signature storage configuration found for myregistry.com:5000/ubuntu:18.04 
DEBU[0000] Looking for TLS certificates and private keys in /etc/docker/certs.d/myregistry.com:5000 
DEBU[0000] GET https://myregistry.com:5000/v2/     
DEBU[0000] Ping https://myregistry.com:5000/v2/ status 404 
DEBU[0000] GET https://myregistry.com:5000/v1/_ping 
DEBU[0000] Ping https://myregistry.com:5000/v1/_ping status 404 
DEBU[0000] Accessing "myregistry.com:5000/ubuntu:18.04" failed: error pinging docker registry myregistry.com:5000: invalid status code from registry 404 (Not Found) 
FATA[0000] Error parsing image name "docker://myregistry.com:5000/ubuntu:18.04": error pinging docker registry myregistry.com:5000: invalid status code from registry 404 (Not Found)

comments.body: Is Docker able to see this image?
Hi @rhatdan . Yes I am able to docker pull and push the image to registry. I am performing skypeo command inside an anchore container.
Might be the networking inside of this container can not get to the registry.  Docker is no running within this container?

@rhatdan Thank you for looking into this. Docker is running in the host perfectly. I am able to perform pull, push command to and from registry. I had the same thouught on if the container was not able to communicate with registry. When I curl to the registry, I do get an html return result so I think, connection-wise networking is good inside container. Please correct me if I am wrong. Here is the output, [anchore@2330fd64fb80 anchore-engine]$ curl https://myregistry.com/ Nexus Repository Manager

> ```
> Ping https://myregistry.com:5000/v2/ status 404
> ```

Most likely there just isn’t a container registry at `myregistry.com:5000`. Maybe some other kind of “registry” is running there, or the host name points to an unexpected server. Alternatively, you could try to connect to this using `curl` and see what the 404 says, if anything.

Hi @mtrmac  yes, I have changed the name of registry.  and you won't be able to browse it from public network because the registry is hosted in private network. Curl command worked fine, it gave me back the html output.  I have attached the output in my last comment. 

anchore@2330fd64fb80 anchore-engine]$ curl https://myregistry.com/
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
<title>Nexus Repository Manager</title>
<meta http-equiv="Content-Type" content="text/html; charset=utf-8"/>
<meta name="description" content="Nexus Repository Manager"/>
<meta http-equiv="X-UA-Compatible"`

Does this look good?
This is the output , that I copied from my container, I changed the hostname to registry.com. The registry that I am hosting is the Nexus.
Does `skopeo inspect` work on the _host_?  It's not yet clear to me whether it's Skopeo or the networking setup of the container Skopeo is running in.

Hi @vrothberg , it is same when I try to use skopeo command in the host as well. I have started seeing this problem, after we have upgraded our docker registry to use self signed certificate. It was working fine without ssl. Does this information help?

@bikashlama541, it helps isolating the error source, yes.

Did you have a look at the logs of your registry?
It was my haproxy error, Had to add a routing rule to accept incoming traffic to docker registry V2 api.
